summary: Security review found one required change: the OpenAI-compatible adapter returns the raw provider HTTP error response body in structured error details, which can leak credentials or request material if a provider or proxy echoes them.
decisions: Requested changes because raw provider HTTP error response text is returned through the reusable adapter error surface.

Accepted the explicit-configuration boundary, absence of process.env reads in services/llm, injected fake-fetch testing, and strict prose-wrapped JSON rejection as security-positive implementation choices.

findings.title: Raw provider HTTP error body is returned to callers
findings.description: The non-OK response path stores await safeReadText(response) in LlmCompletionError.details. OpenAI-compatible providers, gateways, or local proxies can include echoed API keys, authorization failures, prompt snippets, request bodies, or upstream diagnostics in error text. Because this boundary will later run server-side with real credentials and callers may log or forward structured errors, the adapter should not expose raw provider error bodies.
findings.requested_change: Return only sanitized, predictable HTTP error metadata such as status and a generic message, or add a narrowly scoped redaction/sanitization function with tests proving fake secrets and request material are not exposed.

risks: Until fixed, a future server-only caller could log or forward LlmCompletionError.details containing provider/proxy error text with echoed credentials or request material.
The current build emits existing @next/swc-win32-x64-msvc warnings despite successful completion.

tests_run.result: Passed: implementation handoff JSON is valid.
Passed: Next.js production build completed; existing @next/swc-win32-x64-msvc invalid Win32 application warnings were emitted but did not fail the build.

requested_changes: Remove or sanitize raw HTTP provider error bodies from LlmCompletionError.details in the OpenAI-compatible adapter.
Add a focused fake-fetch test for a non-OK provider response whose body contains a fake secret, and assert the returned structured error does not expose that body or secret.
requested_action: Please address the HTTP error detail leak risk, rerun npm test and npm run build, then return the task for security review.
